subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2026, EC, OECD, RSF, Eurostat, Freedom House, IMF COFER) + Belgian official (Statbel, National Bank of Belgium) + Belgian/EU press (VRT, Le Soir, Euronews, ECRE).
A EUROZONE member and host of EU/NATO headquarters; deeply democratic but structurally fragmented along the Flemish-Walloon divide (the federal coalition took ~8 months to form in early 2025).

Standout features: strong institutions, an open trade-hub economy, and excellent healthcare, against a high public-debt load, an asylum-reception crisis, and Brussels/Antwerp narco-violence. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone on the crime/asylum framing.

economic.notes: An open trade-hub economy and sound banks, against a high public-debt load and a structural deficit.

fiscal_state.notes: High debt ~108% GDP and the eurozone's largest deficit (~5.2%), with automatic wage indexation a structural drag and consolidation under-delivering. (IMF Art IV 2026; EC; NBB)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - credible but not sovereign. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: A participant in the euro, the world's second reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Eased from shock peaks, with automatic indexation cushioning households but feeding a competitiveness problem. (NBB; Statbel)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, well-capitalised, and well-supervised. (IMF FSAP; NBB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: An open trade-hub economy - a major port, a chemicals/pharma export base, EU-core logistics - no resource curse. (Eurostat; Statbel)

institutional.notes: An independent judiciary, credible statistics, and a free press, with federal layering slowing execution.

rule_of_law.notes: An independent judiciary that actively checks the government, with narco-corruption pressure the watch-item. (EC Rule of Law 2025; WJP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable, but complex federal/regional layering slows execution. (EC; OECD)

political_social.notes: Strong liberties, against chronic political fragmentation, an asylum-reception crisis, and narco-strains.

political_stability.notes: Deeply democratic but chronically slow to form governments (the federal coalition took ~8 months to early 2025), with a structural Flemish-Walloon split. (VRT; Le Soir)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful EU routes but a severe reception crisis, with the state repeatedly found non-compliant with court orders. (ECRE; AIDA)

social_cohesion.notes: A linguistic divide, a Brussels integration/terrorism legacy, and narco-violence strains. (De Standaard; Le Soir)

demographics.notes: Ageing, with a migration-supported workforce. (Statbel; EC)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable NATO/EU-core member with an open capital account and no direct conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO and EU/NATO-headquarters host and core member, with defence spending lifted to ~2% of GDP from a historically low base. (NATO; VRT)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict, with high territorial security. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare and good infrastructure, against flood risk and urban narco-violence.

climate.notes: Flood risk (the deadly 2021 Wallonia floods), moderate exposure. (EC; NBB)

healthcare.notes: Universal, high-quality, and accessible. (OECD Health at a Glance)

infrastructure.notes: Dense and good, with ageing assets and congestion. (OECD; EC)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate, with Brussels/Antwerp narco-trade violence and a terrorism legacy a watch-item. (Euronews; France24)

disaster_insurance.notes: A developed flood-insurance market with post-2021 reforms. (industry)

summary: A wealthy, open trade-hub economy with strong, independent institutions, a free press, excellent healthcare, and a reliable NATO/EU-core role hosting both blocs' headquarters. The drags are a high public-debt load and the eurozone's largest deficit (with automatic wage indexation a structural feature), chronic political fragmentation along the Flemish-Walloon divide, a severe asylum-reception crisis that courts repeatedly find unlawful, and Brussels/Antwerp narco-violence. Euro membership pools monetary policy at a credible ECB.
